This information is always available in the menu once you close this window.

Welcome to OneRoadTrip

Is it free?

When you book your hotels (even if not the ones we suggest), flights, cars etc. after clicking our links: you won't pay more, and we'll earn a few euros. That's what keeps us going. Either way, OneRoadTrip is 100% free. No false promises, no payment asked at the end.

Can you modify this itinerary?

Yes, you have three ways to do it:

Merge : lets you find nearby itineraries and integrate them into the map and visits.
Edit on map : lets you reorder stops, remove or add new ones.
Full editor : takes you to the next page where you can also edit visits/activities, add more, import your bookings, and print a travel journal.

Trip info

✨ Highlights▾
  • Cape Reinga: sacred Māori site where the Tasman Sea meets the Pacific in a visible swirling current, with the 800-year-old pōhutukawa
  • East Cape: the North Island's remotest road, first place on earth to greet the sunrise
  • Napier: the world's greatest concentration of Art Deco buildings, rebuilt in two years after the 1931 earthquake
  • Milford Sound: one of the world's most spectacular fjords, in UNESCO-listed Fiordland National Park
⏳ How many days▾

Our version is 27 days. The ideal duration falls within a range of 24 to 30 days.

📋 Notes▾

The Wellington-Picton ferry is mandatory between D13 and D14 (approx. 3h). Book ahead in high season. The East Cape road (SH35) is fully sealed but winding; allow plenty of time. The Forgotten World Highway (SH43) includes 12 km of gravel. East Cape lighthouse requires a 20 km return trip on gravel from Te Araroa. Franz Josef glaciers are no longer accessible on foot to the terminal face; helicopter flights available on-site.

💰 Budget▾
✨ Highlights
  • Cape Reinga: sacred Māori site where the Tasman Sea meets the Pacific in a visible swirling current, with the 800-year-old pōhutukawa
  • East Cape: the North Island's remotest road, first place on earth to greet the sunrise
  • Napier: the world's greatest concentration of Art Deco buildings, rebuilt in two years after the 1931 earthquake
  • Milford Sound: one of the world's most spectacular fjords, in UNESCO-listed Fiordland National Park
⏳ How many days

Our version is 27 days. The ideal duration falls within a range of 24 to 30 days.

📋 Notes

The Wellington-Picton ferry is mandatory between D13 and D14 (approx. 3h). Book ahead in high season. The East Cape road (SH35) is fully sealed but winding; allow plenty of time. The Forgotten World Highway (SH43) includes 12 km of gravel. East Cape lighthouse requires a 20 km return trip on gravel from Te Araroa. Franz Josef glaciers are no longer accessible on foot to the terminal face; helicopter flights available on-site.

💰 Budget
✈️ Book a flight 🚗 Rent a car 🏠 Your trips
OneRoadTrip›NZ

The Grand Tour - New Zealand in Its Entirety

📅 27 days📍 27 stops🚗 4060 km⏱ 63h30

Auckland → Paihia → Kaitaia → Hahei → Rotorua → Tolaga Bay → Gisborne → Napier → Whangamomona → New Plymouth → Wellington → Picton → Nelson → Marahau → Takaka → Karamea → Punakaiki → Franz Josef → Wanaka → Queenstown → Te Anau → Mount Cook → Lake Tekapo → Kaikoura → Christchurch

Auckland → Paihia → Kaitaia → Hahei → Rotorua → Tolaga Bay → Gisborne → Napier → Whangamomona → New Plymouth → Wellington → Picton → Nelson → Marahau → Takaka → Karamea → Punakaiki → Franz Josef → Wanaka → Queenstown → Te Anau → Mount Cook → Lake Tekapo → Kaikoura → Christchurch

27 days to cross New Zealand from Cape Reinga to Christchurch without leaving anything out: sacred Far North, isolated East Cape, Art Deco Napier, Forgotten World Highway, wild West Coast, Milford Sound, Hooker Valley and Akaroa's Hector's dolphins. This is the itinerary for travellers who want to understand the country in full, from the black sand beaches of the North Island to the floating icebergs of the South's glacial lakes.

27 days to cross New Zealand from Cape Reinga to Christchurch without leaving anything out: sacred Far North, isolated East Cape, Art Deco Napier, Forgotten World Highway, wild West Coast, Milford Sound, Hooker Valley and Akaroa's Hector's dolphins. This is the itinerary for travellers who want to understand the country in full, from the black sand beaches of the North Island to the floating icebergs of the South's glacial lakes.
📷 Would you like to see the photos before viewing the itinerary?See photos first?
Highlights

The Grand Tour - New Zealand in Its Entirety

1
Auckland
Where volcanic peaks breach a city of sails and twin harbours
Mount Eden AucklandViaduct Harbour AucklandSky Tower AucklandAuckland Waterfront
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car

Where to stay in Auckland

L
$
Book this hotel
Legacy Airedale Hotel Auckland9.1
Budget

Located just 10 minutes' walk from the Sky Tower, Airedale Boutique Suites enjoys a central location on Queen…

Located just 10 minutes' walk from the Sky Tower, Airedale Boutique Suites enjoys a central location on Queen Street and provides guests with a comfortable base in Auckland CBD (Central Business District). Guests enjoy free WiFi. Airedale Boutique Suites was the city's first high-rise building and to this day has kept parts of its original Art Deco elements. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.6 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

T
$$
Book this hotel
The Black Barn9.8
Mid-range

Spa and Wellness Centre: The Black Barn in Auckland offers a spa and wellness centre, providing relaxation…

Spa and Wellness Centre: The Black Barn in Auckland offers a spa and wellness centre, providing relaxation and rejuvenation for all guests. Comfortable Living: This spacious holiday home features one bedroom, a living room, and a fully equipped kitchenette. Guests enjoy air-conditioning, a terrace, and free WiFi in public areas. Leisure Facilities: The property includes a hot tub, barbecue facilities, and a heated pool. Additional amenities include a washing machine, dishwasher, and streaming services. Prime Location: Located 39 km from Auckland Airport, The Black Barn is near attractions such as Eden Park Stadium (23 km) and Sky Tower (26 km). Free on-site private parking is available. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.5 for a two-person trip.

H
$$$
Book this hotel
Hotel DeBrett9.3
Premium

This stylish boutique hotel in the heart of Auckland CBD (Central Business District) is a short walk from the…

This stylish boutique hotel in the heart of Auckland CBD (Central Business District) is a short walk from the Viaduct Harbour and the Sky Tower. It offers free Wi-Fi and an off-site modern fitness centre. All rooms at the Hotel DeBrett have air conditioning, satellite TV. Each room is individually designed, and features New Zealand art and photography. The bathroom includes free EcoStore toiletries. Guests can enjoy cocktails and local beers and wines at the Housebar. Hotel Debrett Auckland is a 5-minute walk from Britomart, and a 15-minute walk from Vector Arena. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.8 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

Paihia
Gateway to the birthplace of a nation, where chiefs and Crown forged history
Waitangi Treaty Grounds PaihiaRussell Bay of IslandsPaihia WaterfrontHaruru Falls Waitangi
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car

Where to stay in Paihia

R
$
Book this hotel
Riverside Homestead9.6
Budget

Elegant Accommodation: Riverside Homestead in Paihia offers a 4-star adults-only homestay experience.

Elegant Accommodation: Riverside Homestead in Paihia offers a 4-star adults-only homestay experience. Guests enjoy a sauna, sun terrace, and lush garden, complemented by a bar and free WiFi. Comfortable Amenities: The property features private check-in and check-out, a paid shuttle service, lounge, and daily housekeeping. Additional amenities include air-conditioning, balcony, and a private bathroom with free toiletries. Delicious Breakfast: A continental or à la carte breakfast is served, including fresh pastries, fruits, and juice. Guests can also enjoy room service and a tour desk for exploring the area. Prime Location: Located 12 km from Bay of Islands Airport, Riverside Homestead is near attractions such as Haruru Falls (2.9 km) and Paihia Harbour (10 km).

B
$$
Book this hotel
Bay Of Islands Beachhouses9.3
Mid-range

Set amongst native trees on the golden sands of Sullivans Beach, just around the corner from Paihia centre,…

Set amongst native trees on the golden sands of Sullivans Beach, just around the corner from Paihia centre, Bay of Islands Beachhouse offers luxurious apartments overlooking the beach and the islands. Each apartment has its own patio and terrace with barbecue facilities and beautiful gardens. All apartments are individually decorated and come with fully equipped kitchens or a kitchenette, flat-screen TV and a CD/DVD player. Paihia Beachhouse has 2 kayaks and a small boat for your use. You can walk down the private pathway onto Sullivans Beach to catch some fish or have a safe swim. All local activities can be booked including day tours, parasailing, jet skiing, canoeing, fishing and sailing.

T
$$$
Book this hotel
Tarlton's Lodge9.6
Premium

Elegant Accommodation: Tarlton's Lodge in Paihia offers adults-only bed and breakfast accommodation with sea…

Elegant Accommodation: Tarlton's Lodge in Paihia offers adults-only bed and breakfast accommodation with sea views. Each room features air-conditioning, a private bathroom, and a terrace. Wellness and Leisure: Guests can relax in the spa and wellness centre, hot tub, or waterpark. The property includes a sun terrace, garden, and outdoor seating area. Free WiFi is available throughout. Convenient Location: Located 23 km from Bay of Islands Airport, the lodge is a 19-minute walk from Paihia Harbour. Nearby attractions include Te Ti Bay Beach (2 km) and Waitangi Treaty Grounds (4.2 km). Guest Favorites: Guests highly rate the host, spa, and scenic location. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.6 for a two-person trip.

Where to stay in Kaitaia

T
$$
Book this hotel
Taipa Views Bed & Breakfast9.7
Mid-range

Spacious Accommodation: Taipa Views Bed & Breakfast in Kaitaia offers spacious family rooms with sea views.

Spacious Accommodation: Taipa Views Bed & Breakfast in Kaitaia offers spacious family rooms with sea views. The property is pet-friendly and includes a sun terrace, garden, and free WiFi. Comfortable Amenities: Guests enjoy air-conditioning, kitchenettes, washing machines, and private bathrooms. Additional features include a dining area, outdoor seating, and barbecue facilities. Delicious Breakfast: A continental breakfast is served with vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free options. Fresh juice and fruits are provided each morning. Highly Rated: Guests appreciate the friendly host, comfortable rooms, and excellent breakfast. The property is highly rated for its excellent service and facilities. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.6 for a two-person trip.

H
$$
Book this hotel
Huriana Place8.8
Mid-range

Comfortable Accommodation: Huriana Place in Kaitaia offers comfortable rooms with private bathrooms,…

Comfortable Accommodation: Huriana Place in Kaitaia offers comfortable rooms with private bathrooms, kitchens, and garden views. Each room includes a dining area, TV, and wardrobe, ensuring a pleasant stay. Essential Facilities: Guests enjoy a beautiful garden, free WiFi, and free on-site private parking. Additional amenities include an outdoor seating area, picnic spots, bicycle parking, and barbecue facilities. Convenient Services: The guest house provides a paid shuttle service, car hire, and outdoor dining area. Free on-site private parking and bicycle parking cater to various needs. Guest Satisfaction: Highly rated by guests, Huriana Place is praised for its beautiful garden, friendly host, and comfortable rooms.

K
$$
Book this hotel
Kauri Lodge Motel8.5
Mid-range

Located in the centre of Kaitaia Town, Kauri Lodge Motel is across the Kaitaia Information Centre, and a…

Located in the centre of Kaitaia Town, Kauri Lodge Motel is across the Kaitaia Information Centre, and a 10-minute drive from the Kaitaia Airport. It offers an outdoor pool, free parking on site and free Wi-Fi access. A free pick-up service from Kaitaia Airport is available. Heated guest rooms have a kitchenette with a microwave, a fridge and tea/coffee making facilities. The en suite bathroom comes with a hairdryer and shower facilities. A self-service laundry is available on site. The staff at the tour desk can assist with making travel arrangements. Car hire is provided for those who wish to explore the area. Guests can enjoy a cooked or continental breakfast in the comfort of their own rooms. Motel Kauri Lodge is a 20-minute drive from Lake Ngatu and the 90 Mile Beach.

Budget

Comfortable Accommodation: Hahei Cottages - Willow or Kauri in Hahei offers a one-bedroom apartment with a…

Comfortable Accommodation: Hahei Cottages - Willow or Kauri in Hahei offers a one-bedroom apartment with a balcony and garden views. The property features a kitchenette, washing machine, and free WiFi. Exceptional Facilities: Guests can enjoy a sun terrace, garden, restaurant, and outdoor seating area. Additional amenities include a minimarket, laundry service, and barbecue facilities. Convenient Location: Located 156 km from Tauranga Airport, the apartment is a 4-minute walk from Hahei Beach and 1 km from Cathedral Cove. Nearby activities include fishing, yoga classes, hiking, cycling, and boating. Highly rated by guests. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.9 for a two-person trip.

$$$
Book this hotel
Hahei Beach Dolphin cottage9.4
Premium

Essential Facilities: Hahei Beach Dolphin cottage in Hahei offers a garden, terrace, and free WiFi.

Essential Facilities: Hahei Beach Dolphin cottage in Hahei offers a garden, terrace, and free WiFi. Guests enjoy sea views, air-conditioning, and a balcony. The property includes a fully equipped kitchen with a barbecue, dining area, and outdoor furniture. Comfortable Living: The holiday home features one bedroom, one bathroom, and a living room. Amenities include a refrigerator, microwave, oven, stovetop, toaster, and free toiletries. Additional features include a dining table, TV, and wardrobe. Prime Location: Located 1.8 km from Hahei Beach and 2.4 km from Cathedral Cove, the property provides easy access to local attractions. Boating is available in the surrounding area. Tauranga Airport is 155 km away. Guests appreciate the scenic views and beach access.

S
$$
Book this hotel
Seabreeze Holiday Park8.5
Mid-range

Seabreeze Holiday Park offers accommodation less than 10 minutes' drive from Hot Water Beach, Cathedral Cove,…

Seabreeze Holiday Park offers accommodation less than 10 minutes' drive from Hot Water Beach, Cathedral Cove, Hahei and Cooks Beach. Free private parking and free WiFi is provided. All of the self-contained accommodation features an outdoor deck, a fully equipped kitchen and a bathroom with shower and toilet. All linen is provided. Check-in is available between 2 PM and 6 PM on the day of your arrival, and check-out is by 10 AM on the day of your departure. If you need to arrange an early or late check-in, please let us know in advance. Guests can enjoy a drink or meal at the on-site seasonal restaurant, The Family, while the kids play at the playground. Seabreeze Holiday Park is 55 minutes' drive from Coromandel Town and 2 hours' drive from Auckland.

Additional facilities include a private check-in and check-out service, daily housekeeping, and an outdoor seating area. Prime Location: Located 1.9 km from Bell Block Beach and 6 km from New Plymouth Airport, the property is near attractions such as Pukekura Park and Yarrow Stadium. Highly rated for room cleanliness, location with a view, and room comfort. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.6 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

City Living10
Mid-range

Prime Location: City Living in New Plymouth offers a convenient apartment with a private beach area and free…

Prime Location: City Living in New Plymouth offers a convenient apartment with a private beach area and free WiFi. Fitzroy Beach is a 5-minute walk away, while Puke Ariki lies 1.3 km nearby. Comfortable Amenities: The apartment features air-conditioning, a washing machine, and a private bathroom. Guests can enjoy free on-site private parking, laundry service, and an outdoor seating area. Local Attractions: Explore Pukekura Park, Len Lye Centre, and Govett Brewster Art Gallery within walking distance. Yarrow Stadium is 3.5 km away, while Port Taranaki lies 4.8 km from the property. Activities and Surroundings: Enjoy fishing, skiing, windsurfing, and hiking in the surrounding area. New Plymouth Airport is 11 km from the apartment.

Book this hotel
One Burgess Hill9.3
Premium

One Burgess Hill offers a choice of 15 self-contained units overlooking the stunning Waiwhakaiho River and…

One Burgess Hill offers a choice of 15 self-contained units overlooking the stunning Waiwhakaiho River and native landscape. It provides modern rooms, some of which feature a wood burning fire and private outdoor spa. New Plymouth city centre is a 5-minute drive from One Burgesshill. New Plymouth Airport is only a 15-minute drive away. Each studio and apartment features heating, flat-screen satellite TV. They are equipped with a microwave, a mini-fridge and an electric stove top with 2 hobs. Some come with a spa bath and air-conditioning. Guest laundry and BBQ facilities are available. Breakfast and dinner can be delivered to your room. It is a 5-minute drive to local restaurants. Our modern restaurant café is overlooking the beautiful Waiwakaiho River and native bush.

$

Pacific View Bed and Breakfast9.4
Budget

Elegant Accommodation: Pacific View Bed and Breakfast in Wellington offers adults-only rooms with sea views,…

Elegant Accommodation: Pacific View Bed and Breakfast in Wellington offers adults-only rooms with sea views, private bathrooms, and modern amenities. Each room includes a bathrobe, free toiletries, and a TV. Comfortable Facilities: Guests enjoy free WiFi, private check-in and check-out services, daily housekeeping, and free on-site parking. Additional amenities include baths with walk-in showers and carpeted floors. Prime Location: Located 3 km from Wellington Airport, the property is near attractions such as Ataturk Memorial (less than 1 km) and Lyall Bay Surf Beach (2.8 km). Highly rated for its host, scenic views, and breakfast. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.5 for a two-person trip.

T
$$
Book this hotel
The Marion Hostel9.3
Mid-range

Located in the heart of the city of Wellington,The Marion Hostel offers a range of backpacker accommodation …

Located in the heart of the city of Wellington,The Marion Hostel offers a range of backpacker accommodation and features a rooftop terrace. It is just a 1-minute walk from Cuba Street where you will find a variety of restaurants, cafes and bars. Free high speed unlimited WiFi is offered throughout the property. The property offers a choice of rooms including spacious dormitory rooms and private rooms, including one private room with an en suite. All rooms offer complimentary towels, hairdryer and toiletries. The dormitory rooms include reading lights, USB plugs, privacy curtains and lockers. Linen is provided for all rooms. Guests have access to shared fully equipped kitchens and dining area as well as two spacious guest lounge areas with large TV’s with Netflix.

O
$$$
Book this hotel
Ohtel Wellington9.1
Premium

Ohtel Wellington, located in Oriental Bay, offers guests a 5-star experience from the personalised service…

Ohtel Wellington, located in Oriental Bay, offers guests a 5-star experience from the personalised service and luxurious accommodation. The rooms are individually furnished with mid-century elegance and fuse international class with urban chic. Adjacent to Wellington’s entertainment precinct and seaside promenade, Ohtel Wellington is close to major attractions and many theatres, restaurants and entertainment venues. The boutique hotel offers rooms with Smart TV's, free WiFi, air-con, and marble bathrooms with kiwi made amenities. Mini-bars are stocked with a selection of boutique New Zealand products with complimentary tea and coffee. As well as providing room service and breakfast, Ohtel Pantry offers wine, coffee and small bites focusing on local ingredients.

Delorenzo Studio Apartments offers a heated outdoor pool, on-site parking and free Wi-Fi access. Studios come with a kitchenette, laundry facilities and a private bathroom. The attached kitchenette features a stove, a microwave and tea/coffee making facilities. There is a barbecue area available for guests’ use. Baggage storage is provided. The property offers an in-room dining service for dinner. Studio Apartments Delorenzo is 250 metres from the centre of Nelson. It is a 12-minute drive from Nelson Golf Links and a 15-minute drive from the Nelson Airport.

Located at the heart of the Central Otago wine region and just a 20-minute drive from Queenstown Airport,…

Located at the heart of the Central Otago wine region and just a 20-minute drive from Queenstown Airport, Gibbston Valley Lodge and Spa offers luxury accommodation, 5-star personal service, an on-site restaurant and a Day spa. The 24 private villas feature views over the organic vineyards and the mountains of Gibbston. All villas are air conditioned and include a spacious and luxurious indoor living space. They open onto a private, secluded, covered deck and sunny courtyard. The large bathroom has a walk-in shower, separate bath and dual basins as well as premium, locally made beauty products. Guests will be able to enjoy a wide variety of experiential packages, tailored to their needs and interests.

Just 5 minutes’ walk from Lake Te Anau, The Village Inn features a restaurant, a garden and a bar with a…

$$

Aoraki Court Motel9.2
Mid-range

Aoraki Court Motel is a modern accommodation located in Aoraki Mount Cook National Park.

Aoraki Court Motel is a modern accommodation located in Aoraki Mount Cook National Park. All units feature great views of surrounding mountains and Mount Sefton. There is free on-site parking and free Wi-Fi. The heated guest rooms come with double glazed windows and a flat-screen TV. Each has a patio with mountain views. All rooms also have a microwave, a fridge and kitchenware. Self-service guest laundry is available. The property can provide guests with free hiking maps and daily newspaper. Union Pay credit cards are accepted. Aoraki Court Motel is a 3-minute walk from various dining options at Mount Cook Village. It is 210 metres from Sir Edmund Hillary Alpine Centre and 450 metres from Aoraki Mount Cook National Park Visitor Centre. Aoraki Mount Cook Airport is 6.5 km away.

Write to us

A question, a suggestion, a photo you'd like to see, a personalized itinerary idea… tell us everything, we read it all.

Day 1

Auckland

Where volcanic peaks breach a city of sails and twin harbours
Mount Eden AucklandViaduct Harbour AucklandSky Tower AucklandAuckland Waterfront
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Auckland is built across 53 volcanic cones - no other city on earth can claim that. Climb to the crater rim of Mount Eden (Maungawhau) at 196 m elevation: the panorama over the isthmus, both harbours and the skyline is worth every step. At the bottom of the perfectly preserved crater, ancient Māori cultivation terraces are still visible from the rim.

⏱ 60 min
2

Head down to Viaduct Harbour, a former fishing basin redeveloped in 2000 for the America's Cup. Today it's 30 restaurants and bars lining the marina, with direct views of superyachts. The New Zealand Maritime Museum, set on the waterfront, traces the history from early Polynesian explorers through to the great sailing regattas - Auckland genuinely deserves its nickname 'City of Sails'.

⏱ 90 min
3

Wander through the Ponsonby neighbourhood, ten minutes from the CBD: restored Victorian villas, art galleries, independent cafés and local boutiques. It's Auckland's liveliest quarter for understanding contemporary Kiwi culture.

⏱ 60 min

Activities

Head up the Sky Tower for the 360° view - the tower rises to 328 m and remains the tallest structure in the southern hemisphere. The brave can sign up for SkyJump (guided freefall from the top). For everyone else, the glass-walled restaurant on the 52nd floor is more than enough.

⏱ 60 min

In the evening, explore the waterfront from Viaduct to Wynyard Quarter: oyster bars, seafood restaurants and lively terraces facing the marina. It's one of the city's best spots for dinner before an early start tomorrow.

⏱ 90 min

Where to stay in Auckland

L
$
Book this hotel
Legacy Airedale Hotel Auckland9.1
Budget

Located just 10 minutes' walk from the Sky Tower, Airedale Boutique Suites enjoys a central location on Queen…

Located just 10 minutes' walk from the Sky Tower, Airedale Boutique Suites enjoys a central location on Queen Street and provides guests with a comfortable base in Auckland CBD (Central Business District). Guests enjoy free WiFi. Airedale Boutique Suites was the city's first high-rise building and to this day has kept parts of its original Art Deco elements. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.6 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

It offers free Wi-Fi and an off-site modern fitness centre. All rooms at the Hotel DeBrett have air conditioning, satellite TV. Each room is individually designed, and features New Zealand art and photography. The bathroom includes free EcoStore toiletries. Guests can enjoy cocktails and local beers and wines at the Housebar. Hotel Debrett Auckland is a 5-minute walk from Britomart, and a 15-minute walk from Vector Arena. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.8 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

Day 2

Paihia

Gateway to the birthplace of a nation, where chiefs and Crown forged history
Waitangi Treaty Grounds PaihiaRussell Bay of IslandsPaihia WaterfrontHaruru Falls Waitangi
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Waitangi Treaty Grounds, 2 km from Paihia, is where it all began: on 6 February 1840, 46 Māori chiefs and representatives of the British Crown signed the Treaty of Waitangi here, the founding document of the New Zealand nation. The Treaty House, delivered as a kit from Sydney in 1834, was the residence of British Resident James Busby. Inside Te Whare Rūnanga, the carved whare built for the 1940 centenary, the ornaments represent tribes from across New Zealand. The ceremonial waka Ngātokimatawhaorua measures 35 metres and is launched every Waitangi Day.

⏱ 120 min
2

Take the ferry from Paihia wharf to Russell (15 minutes), New Zealand's unofficial first capital. This is where whalers, traders and missionaries disembarked from the 1830s onwards. Christ Church (1836) is the country's oldest church - its walls still bear bullet marks from the 1845 wars. Pompallier Mission House, built in 1842, was the first Catholic establishment in the entire Asia-Pacific region.

⏱ 90 min

Activities

Join the Māori cultural evening at Waitangi Treaty Grounds: pōwhiri (welcome ceremony), haka, poi and a traditional hāngī meal - vegetables and meat slow-cooked in an earth oven. Available October to April, booking essential.

⏱ 150 min

Bay of Islands sunset cruise: the 144 islands scattered across the bay are accessible by catamaran from Paihia. It's one of New Zealand's most prized sailing spots, and the late afternoon light on the forested islands is particularly beautiful.

⏱ 120 min

Where to stay in Paihia

R
$
Book this hotel
Riverside Homestead9.6
Budget

Additional amenities include air-conditioning, balcony, and a private bathroom with free toiletries. Prime Location: Located 12 km from Bay of Islands Airport, Riverside Homestead is near attractions such as Haruru Falls (2.9 km) and Paihia Harbour (10 km).

B
$$
Book this hotel
Bay Of Islands Beachhouses9.3
Mid-range

Set amongst native trees on the golden sands of Sullivans Beach, just around the corner from Paihia centre,…

Set amongst native trees on the golden sands of Sullivans Beach, just around the corner from Paihia centre, Bay of Islands Beachhouse offers luxurious apartments overlooking the beach and the islands. Each apartment has its own patio and terrace with barbecue facilities and beautiful gardens. All apartments are individually decorated and come with fully equipped kitchens or a kitchenette, flat-screen TV and a CD/DVD player. Paihia Beachhouse has 2 kayaks and a small boat for your use. You can walk down the private pathway onto Sullivans Beach to catch some fish or have a safe swim. All local activities can be booked including day tours, parasailing, jet skiing, canoeing, fishing and sailing.

Day 3

Kaitaia

Springboard to the sacred northern cape where oceans collide
Cape Reinga LighthouseNinety Mile Beach Te PakiTe Paki Sand DunesSpirits Bay Cape Reinga
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Cape Reinga - Te Rerenga Wairua in Māori, 'the leaping place of spirits' - is the most sacred site in all of New Zealand. According to Māori tradition, the souls of the departed dive from the 800-year-old pōhutukawa clinging to the rocks to return to Hawaiki, the ancestral homeland. A one-kilometre path from the car park leads to the lighthouse and the famous headland where the Tasman Sea and Pacific Ocean collide in a visible swirling current. This isn't the northernmost point - that honour belongs to Surville Cliffs, inaccessible to the right on the horizon.

⏱ 90 min
2

Ninety Mile Beach is actually only 88 km (55 miles) long - its name comes from European horsemen who estimated their horses covered 30 miles per day, forgetting that sand slows the pace. The beach is officially a national highway: 4x4 vehicles can drive on it, and tour buses regularly venture out between tides. Stop at Spirits Bay, a sheltered beach east of the cape, quieter and often deserted.

⏱ 60 min

Activities

Sandboarding on the Te Paki dunes: north of Ninety Mile Beach, immense sand dunes rise like desert mountains - a completely unexpected landscape in New Zealand. Local operators offer board hire. Allow about 20 minutes to climb for memorable descents.

⏱ 90 min

The road to Cape Reinga follows the Aupōuri Peninsula, a narrow tongue of land with the Tasman Sea to the west and the Pacific to the east. Make photo stops at Rarawa Beach, reputed to have the whitest sand in all of New Zealand, and at Tapotupotu Bay beach, sheltered and perfect for a swim.

⏱ 90 min

Where to stay in Kaitaia

$$

Located in the centre of Kaitaia Town, Kauri Lodge Motel is across the Kaitaia Information Centre, and a 10-minute drive from the Kaitaia Airport. A self-service laundry is available on site. The staff at the tour desk can assist with making travel arrangements. Car hire is provided for those who wish to explore the area. Day 4

Hahei

Where golden sands meet cathedral arches carved by Pacific waves
Cathedral Cove Hahei CoromandelHot Water Beach CoromandelHahei Beach CoromandelCathedral Cove Marine Reserve
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Cathedral Cove - Mautohe - owes its English name to James Cook who, sailing through Mercury Bay in November 1769, thought the natural arch resembled a cathedral roof. Access is on foot (45 min from Grange Road, fully sealed marked track) or by water taxi from Hahei Beach. The giant arch connects two separate coves, and Te Hoho Rock, the stone pinnacle in the middle of the bay, is visible from inside. The 9 km² marine reserve surrounding the site is the country's sixth. Warning: there is a real rockfall risk under the arch - don't linger beneath it.

⏱ 120 min
2

Hot Water Beach is a geological curiosity: buried ancient lava flow heats underground water that rises through the sand at temperatures reaching 64°C. The active zone is accessible only two hours either side of low tide. You dig your own natural spa with a spade (hire available at nearby cafés), then cool off in the ocean waves. The beach receives over 150,000 visitors per year - arrive early.

⏱ 90 min

Activities

Sea kayaking from Hahei Beach to Cathedral Cove: this is the most magical way to approach the cove, gliding under the arch at low tide. Local operators offer 3-hour guided trips including snorkelling stops in the marine reserve.

⏱ 180 min

In the evening, stroll along Hahei Beach itself, a long golden sand beach with views to the Mercury Islands. The Ngāti Hei people inhabited these shores from 1350 AD and numerous pā (forts) are still visible on the headlands.

⏱ 60 min

Where to stay in Hahei

H
$
Book this hotel

If you need to arrange an early or late check-in, please let us know in advance. Guests can enjoy a drink or meal at the on-site seasonal restaurant, The Family, while the kids play at the playground. Seabreeze Holiday Park is 55 minutes' drive from Coromandel Town and 2 hours' drive from Auckland.

Day 5

Rotorua

Earth breathes fire beneath a lake, while culture pulses through steam
Wai-O-Tapu Thermal Wonderland RotoruaLady Knox Geyser RotoruaChampagne Pool Wai-O-TapuWhakarewarewa Forest Redwoods Rotorua
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Wai-O-Tapu - 'sacred waters' - lies 27 km south of Rotorua and covers 18 km² of active geothermal zone in the Reporoa caldera. Lady Knox Geyser erupts every morning at precisely 10:15 am - arrive by 9:45 am for a good spot. After the geyser, three walking circuits (45 min to 90 min) lead to Champagne Pool (57°C, ringed with orange sulphur deposits), the Artist's Palette, bubbling mud pools and Devil's Bath, a toxic chartreuse-green pool from sulphur and ferrous salt deposits. Allow at least 2 hours minimum.

⏱ 120 min
2

The Redwoods Whakarewarewa Forest, on the edge of Rotorua, is a California redwood forest planted from 1901 as forestry trials. The trees have reached impressive heights - some exceed 60 m - and the understory is carpeted with silver ferns. Cycling and walking tracks traverse the entire forest. The Redwood Treewalk (suspended walkway between the canopy) is a separate paid activity.

⏱ 75 min

Activities

Māori cultural evening at Tamaki village: warriors collect visitors by bus, divide them into 'tribes' and welcome them with a full pōwhiri including the wero (challenge) ceremony. You learn poi handling, haka, traditional games, before a hāngī served in the hall. Tamaki is renowned for its passionate guides and authenticity.

⏱ 180 min

Relax in the Polynesian Spa pools on the shore of Lake Rotorua: 26 pools at different temperatures fed by natural geothermal springs. The waters have distinct mineral properties depending on the source - some high in sulphur, others alkaline.

⏱ 90 min

Where to stay in Rotorua

$

Day 6

Tolaga Bay

A pier stretching into forever on a coast time forgot
Tolaga Bay Wharf New ZealandOpotiki East CapeCook's Cove Walkway Tolaga BaySH35 East Cape Road
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

The East Cape coast begins at Ōpōtiki, where SH35 splits from the main highway to follow the Bay of Plenty shore for 330 km of winding road. This is where the black sand beaches appear - the dark sand comes from eroded volcanic rock from the hinterland. Slow down at every viewpoint: the coast alternates between cliffs, river mouths and isolated bays where nobody stops.

⏱ 60 min
2

Tolaga Bay Wharf is New Zealand's longest pier at 660 metres. Built in 1929 to allow steamships to load wool and meat without running aground on the shallows, it was the economic artery of the entire coast. Walk to the end: the waters are crystal clear, you can see fish swimming between the piles, and the view of the white cliffs is striking. Some locals jump from the end at high tide.

⏱ 60 min

Activities

Hike to Cook's Cove: from the car park at Tolaga Bay entrance, this 2.5 km one-way trail leads to a secluded cove with historical panels recounting James Cook's 1769 anchorage. The captain needed fresh water and timber after five weeks at sea - he stayed six days. Spectacular cliff views from the headland.

⏱ 120 min

Sunset over the bay from the wharf or Tolaga Bay beach: the black sand beaches ignite at sunset, and the sound of Pacific waves echoes in a silence that contrasts completely with the rest of the circuit. This is the Kiwi Far East - one of the remotest and quietest corners of the country.

⏱ 60 min

Where to stay in Tolaga Bay

🗺️ See hotels on the map
🚗Drive to next stop: 55 km · 50min
Day 7

Gisborne

First light touches earth here, before anywhere else on the planet
East Cape Lighthouse Te AraroaRaukokore Anglican Church East CapeTe Araroa Pohutukawa TreeGisborne Tairawhiti Museum
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Christ Church Raukokore is an isolated Anglican church built in 1894 on a small rocky headland battered by the waves of Papatea Bay. It's so small and exposed it seems to rise from the sea. The New Zealand Automobile Association ranked it among the region's 101 must-see sites - penguins sometimes nest under the floorboards. It's one of the most iconic images of the East Cape road.

⏱ 30 min
2

At Te Araroa, on the roadside, stands New Zealand's oldest pōhutukawa - 600 years old, with tentacular branches covering over 20 metres in diameter. The pōhutukawa is New Zealand's Christmas tree: it blazes with crimson red flowers in December. From Te Araroa, a 22 km track leads to East Cape lighthouse, relocated here in 1922 from the small offshore island (the original island was too unstable). You must climb 700 steps from the car park to reach the lighthouse - the Pacific view from the top is worth every step.

⏱ 150 min
3

Gisborne is the first city in the world to greet the sun each morning. The Tairawhiti Museum, by the river, displays Māori and colonial collections including the helm from the Star of Canada, wrecked here in 1912. The Hawke's Bay vineyards begin on the city outskirts - Gisborne produces notably reputed chardonnay.

⏱ 60 min

Activities

Swimming and surfing on Gisborne's beaches: Wainui Beach, 5 km north of town, is one of the North Island's most renowned surf beaches. Locals gather there in late afternoon. The Pacific view from the dunes at sunset is particularly beautiful.

⏱ 90 min

Where to stay in Gisborne

Day 8

Napier

Phoenix risen in Art Deco glory from the ashes of catastrophe
Napier Art Deco precinct Emerson StreetNational Tobacco Company Building NapierMarine Parade NapierPania of the Reef statue Napier
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

On 3 February 1931 at 10:47 am, a magnitude 7.8 earthquake levelled central Napier in two and a half minutes, killing 256 people. The fires that followed finished destroying the commercial districts. The reconstruction, conducted during the depths of the Great Depression in less than two years, naturally adopted the Art Deco style then in vogue, especially since its reinforced concrete buildings better withstood earthquakes. Napier now has the world's greatest concentration of Art Deco buildings - 144 buildings constructed between 1931 and 1937 out of 160 total remain intact. The National Tobacco Company Building (1933), with its arched entrance, dome and stained glass adorned with raupo, is the most photographed structure.

⏱ 120 min
2

Marine Parade runs along the seafront for 2 km, lined with palms and Norfolk Island pines planted on land raised by the earthquake - the tremor literally created 4,000 hectares of new land by lifting the bay floor 2.7 m. The statue of Pania of the Reef, a local legendary figure, has stood at the water's edge since 1954 - she is to Napier what the Little Mermaid is to Copenhagen.

⏱ 60 min

Activities

Guided Art Deco walking tour with the Art Deco Trust: guides dressed in 1930s costume take you through shopping streets and interior lanes to decode the facades, chevron patterns and mosaics. The tour lasts about 1h30. If you pass through in February, the Tremains Art Deco Festival transforms Napier into a film set with vintage cars and residents in period costume.

⏱ 90 min

Excursion to Hawke's Bay vineyards: 15 minutes from Napier, the region is New Zealand's second-largest wine area by surface. It specialises in chardonnay, pinot gris and red blends. Several estates offer on-site tastings in the afternoon.

⏱ 120 min

Where to stay in Napier

Day 9

Whangamomona

Where a republic was born from a joke and a goat became president
Whangamomona Hotel Forgotten World HighwayTaumarunui Forgotten World HighwayMoki Tunnel Forgotten World HighwayTangarakau Gorge Highway 43
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

The Forgotten World Highway (SH43) is New Zealand's oldest touring route - 150 km between Taumarunui and Stratford, built over more than 50 years. The road crosses four passes, traverses the Tangarakau Gorge and plunges into a single-lane tunnel - Moki Tunnel (183 m) - before arriving at Whangamomona. There are no petrol stations along these 150 km. Fill up at Taumarunui. The 12 km of gravel are easily drivable in a normal car.

⏱ 90 min
2

Whangamomona, founded in 1895, declared itself an independent Republic in November 1989 after an administrative redistricting split the village between two regions without consulting residents. What started as a joke in the local hotel bar became a biennial celebration attracting up to 5,000 people. Successive presidents have included a goat, a poodle and a human. Buy your Republic of Whangamomona passport at the Whangamomona Hotel bar to ensure trouble-free transit.

⏱ 60 min

Activities

Detour to Mount Damper Falls: midway between Taumarunui and Whangamomona, a 3 km track leads to the North Island's tallest falls (88 m by some sources). The path passes through the former community of Aotuhia, abandoned in the 1940s after total isolation from floods.

⏱ 90 min

Rail cart on Forgotten World Adventures: self-guided electric rail carts allow you to travel the historic railway line between Taumarunui and the remote Tokirima bush. A quirky way to see the landscape.

⏱ 120 min

Where to stay in Whangamomona

$

Day 10

New Plymouth

Where a perfect volcanic cone commands the Tasman horizon
Mount Taranaki New PlymouthCoastal Walkway New PlymouthGovett-Brewster Art Gallery New PlymouthPuke Ariki Museum New Plymouth
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Mount Taranaki (2,518 m) is one of the world's most symmetrical volcanoes - its silhouette is so perfect it doubled for Fuji in the film The Last Samurai. It stands alone in a dairy plain, with no mountain range, making it visible from great distances. The Pouakai Circuit (2 days) and Around the Mountain Track (4 days) are the major hikes in Egmont National Park. From New Plymouth, Egmont Road leads to the Visitor Centre at 900 m elevation where several shorter trails begin.

⏱ 90 min
2

The New Plymouth Coastal Walkway follows 13 km of coast between black lava rocks and the Tasman Sea. Free, accessible by bike or foot, with views of Mount Taranaki on clear days. The Len Lye Centre, a kinetic art installation along the walkway, is the work of an internationally renowned New Zealand artist.

⏱ 75 min
3

Puke Ariki, New Plymouth's museum, sits on the site of the region's first pā fort. It houses Māori and colonial collections relating to the 19th-century Taranaki Wars, a period when lands were confiscated en masse from local iwi.

⏱ 60 min

Activities

Hike to Pouakai Tarn from the North Egmont Visitor Centre: this small mountain lake, 2 hours' walk one way from the car park, offers a perfect reflection of Taranaki in calm weather - one of New Zealand's most famous photographs. Leave early in the morning to find still waters.

⏱ 240 min

Where to stay in New Plymouth

T
$

The Lily Inn9.8
Budget

Spacious Accommodation: The Lily Inn in New Plymouth offers spacious, recently renovated adults-only rooms with sea views. Each room includes a kitchenette, private bathroom, and outdoor patio. Mid-range

Prime Location: City Living in New Plymouth offers a convenient apartment with a private beach area and free…

Prime Location: City Living in New Plymouth offers a convenient apartment with a private beach area and free WiFi. Fitzroy Beach is a 5-minute walk away, while Puke Ariki lies 1.3 km nearby. Comfortable Amenities: The apartment features air-conditioning, a washing machine, and a private bathroom. Guests can enjoy free on-site private parking, laundry service, and an outdoor seating area. Local Attractions: Explore Pukekura Park, Len Lye Centre, and Govett Brewster Art Gallery within walking distance. Yarrow Stadium is 3.5 km away, while Port Taranaki lies 4.8 km from the property. Activities and Surroundings: Enjoy fishing, skiing, windsurfing, and hiking in the surrounding area. New Plymouth Airport is 11 km from the apartment.

One Burgess Hill offers a choice of 15 self-contained units overlooking the stunning Waiwhakaiho River and…

One Burgess Hill offers a choice of 15 self-contained units overlooking the stunning Waiwhakaiho River and native landscape. It provides modern rooms, some of which feature a wood burning fire and private outdoor spa. New Plymouth city centre is a 5-minute drive from One Burgesshill. They are equipped with a microwave, a mini-fridge and an electric stove top with 2 hobs. Some come with a spa bath and air-conditioning. Guest laundry and BBQ facilities are available. Breakfast and dinner can be delivered to your room. It is a 5-minute drive to local restaurants. Our modern restaurant café is overlooking the beautiful Waiwakaiho River and native bush.

Day 11

Wellington

Wind-whipped capital where creativity thrives between harbour and hills
Te Papa Museum Wellington WaterfrontMount Victoria Lookout WellingtonCuba Street WellingtonWellington Cable Car
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Te Papa Tongarewa - 'the place of treasures of this land' - is New Zealand's national museum, opened in 1998 on the Wellington waterfront. The 36,000 m² building, set on lead-rubber seismic isolators, houses over two million objects: Māori taonga, natural fauna including the famous colossal squid, art collections and the Gallipoli exhibition created by Weta Workshop with figures 2.4 times life-size. Allow at least 3 hours minimum.

⏱ 180 min
2

The Mount Victoria lookout (196 m) offers the best view over Wellington, its harbour and the entrance to Cook Strait. This is where Peter Jackson filmed several scenes from the Lord of the Rings trilogy. You can drive up or walk in 30 minutes from the centre. Cuba Street below, with its Bucket Fountain and alternative shops, is the capital's liveliest artery.

⏱ 75 min

Activities

Ride the cable car from Lambton Quay up to the Botanic Garden: Wellington's botanical gardens (26 hectares) descend in terraces to the city centre with panoramic harbour views. The cable car has operated since 1902. At the top, the Carter Observatory completes the visit.

⏱ 90 min

Dinner on Cuba Street: Wellington's bohemian artery concentrates the capital's best independent restaurants, cocktail bars and cafés. Wellington's culinary scene is renowned throughout the country - proportionally to its size, the city has more cafés per capita than any other city in New Zealand.

⏱ 120 min

Where to stay in Wellington

P
$
Book this hotel
Pacific View Bed and Breakfast9.4
Budget

Elegant Accommodation: Pacific View Bed and Breakfast in Wellington offers adults-only rooms with sea views,…

Elegant Accommodation: Pacific View Bed and Breakfast in Wellington offers adults-only rooms with sea views, private bathrooms, and modern amenities. Each room includes a bathrobe, free toiletries, and a TV. Comfortable Facilities: Guests enjoy free WiFi, private check-in and check-out services, daily housekeeping, and free on-site parking. Additional amenities include baths with walk-in showers and carpeted floors. Prime Location: Located 3 km from Wellington Airport, the property is near attractions such as Ataturk Memorial (less than 1 km) and Lyall Bay Surf Beach (2.8 km). Highly rated for its host, scenic views, and breakfast. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.5 for a two-person trip.

T
$$
Book this hotel
The Marion Hostel9.3
Mid-range

Located in the heart of the city of Wellington,The Marion Hostel offers a range of backpacker accommodation …

Located in the heart of the city of Wellington,The Marion Hostel offers a range of backpacker accommodation and features a rooftop terrace. It is just a 1-minute walk from Cuba Street where you will find a variety of restaurants, cafes and bars. Free high speed unlimited WiFi is offered throughout the property. The property offers a choice of rooms including spacious dormitory rooms and private rooms, including one private room with an en suite. All rooms offer complimentary towels, hairdryer and toiletries. The dormitory rooms include reading lights, USB plugs, privacy curtains and lockers. Linen is provided for all rooms. Guests have access to shared fully equipped kitchens and dining area as well as two spacious guest lounge areas with large TV’s with Netflix.

O
$$$

Ohtel Wellington9.1
Premium

Ohtel Wellington, located in Oriental Bay, offers guests a 5-star experience from the personalised service…

Ohtel Wellington, located in Oriental Bay, offers guests a 5-star experience from the personalised service and luxurious accommodation. The rooms are individually furnished with mid-century elegance and fuse international class with urban chic. Adjacent to Wellington’s entertainment precinct and seaside promenade, Ohtel Wellington is close to major attractions and many theatres, restaurants and entertainment venues. The boutique hotel offers rooms with Smart TV's, free WiFi, air-con, and marble bathrooms with kiwi made amenities. Mini-bars are stocked with a selection of boutique New Zealand products with complimentary tea and coffee. As well as providing room service and breakfast, Ohtel Pantry offers wine, coffee and small bites focusing on local ingredients.

Day 12

Wellington

Wind-whipped capital where creativity thrives between harbour and hills
Zealandia Ecosanctuary Wellington KaroriOld St Paul's Cathedral WellingtonNew Zealand Parliament Wellington BeehiveWellington Waterfront
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Zealandia Te Māra a Tāne is the world's first fully fenced, predator-free urban sanctuary. Its 225 hectares, 4 km from the CBD, shelter 40 endemic bird species including kiwi, tuatara (prehistoric reptile unchanged for 200 million years), kākā and takahē - believed extinct until its rediscovery in 1948. Since the sanctuary's opening in 1999, 18 species have been reintroduced. The night tour allows observation of kiwi in their natural element.

⏱ 180 min
2

Old St Paul's Cathedral (1866) is a Gothic cathedral built entirely of local pine timber with kauri reinforcements - a world-unique technique dictated by the absence of available building stone during colonisation. The interior with its coloured stained glass and exposed timber frame has remarkable acoustics. It's one of New Zealand's most precious heritage buildings.

⏱ 45 min
3

The New Zealand Parliament with its cylindrical tower nicknamed 'the Beehive' (1977, designed by architect Basil Spence) is one of the country's most recognisable buildings. Free guided tours run several times daily.

⏱ 60 min

Activities

Zealandia night tour with guide: guides equipped with red-light torches lead groups searching for kiwi, tuatara and glow-worms roaming free in the forest. The sanctuary's transformation after nightfall is spectacular - nocturnal birds take over the pathways.

⏱ 120 min

Where to stay in Wellington

$

Day 13

Picton

Where ferries thread through drowned valleys of impossible beauty
Interislander Ferry Wellington PictonQueen Charlotte Sound PictonPicton Harbour MarlboroughQueen Charlotte Drive
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

The Interislander ferry (3h Wellington to Picton) is a spectacle in itself: the Cook Strait crossing opens onto the Marlborough Sounds, a labyrinth of 1,500 km of submerged coastline created by rising waters 14,000 years ago at the end of the last ice age. Mountains plunge directly into the water from heights of several hundred metres, and successive sea arms reveal themselves at each turn. Settle on the outer deck.

⏱ 180 min
2

From Picton, head out on the Queen Charlotte Drive, a 35 km scenic road winding between forested ridges and the coves of Queen Charlotte Sound to Havelock. Several lookouts open onto views that photos don't do justice. Havelock is nicknamed 'the green-lipped mussel capital of the world' - a seafood meal is mandatory here.

⏱ 120 min

Activities

Cruise in Queen Charlotte Sound from Picton: water taxis and excursion boats offer half-day trips into the most remote arms of the Sound, with dolphin, seal and seabird watching. James Cook anchored at Ship Cove - accessible only by sea - five times between 1770 and 1777; his memorial is still visible there.

⏱ 180 min

Where to stay in Picton

P
$
Book this hotel
Picton Moana View Hideaway9.7
Budget

Modern Comforts: Picton Moana View Hideaway in Picton offers a recently renovated apartment with one bedroom…

Modern Comforts: Picton Moana View Hideaway in Picton offers a recently renovated apartment with one bedroom and one bathroom. The property features air-conditioning, a kitchenette, balcony, and free WiFi. Outdoor Spaces: Guests can enjoy a garden, terrace, and outdoor seating area. The apartment includes a picnic area and free bicycles for exploring the surroundings. Convenient Amenities: The apartment provides free off-site parking, private check-in and check-out, daily housekeeping, and bicycle parking. Additional amenities include a walk-in shower, TV, and free toiletries. Local Attractions: Located 11 km from Picton Aerodrome, the property is ideal for hiking and cycling enthusiasts. The surrounding area offers scenic views of the garden and mountains.

Activities such as hiking and cycling are available nearby. Guest Favorites: Guests highly rate the host, garden, and breakfast provided by the property.

Day 14

Nelson

Sunshine capital where artisans craft and hops flourish in golden light
Nelson City Centre New ZealandTahunanui Beach NelsonCentre of New Zealand Monument NelsonNelson Market
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Nelson is New Zealand's sunniest city - Blenheim sometimes competes, but Nelson usually wins over the year. The city centre with its art galleries, Saturday morning market (one of the country's liveliest) and independent cafés is particularly pleasant to explore. Christ Church Cathedral dominates the central hill.

⏱ 90 min
2

Climb to the Centre of New Zealand Monument, the official geodetic point from which all topographic maps of the country were drawn. The climb from Queens Gardens takes 20 minutes on foot through bush. The view over Tasman Bay and surrounding ranges is remarkable.

⏱ 60 min
3

Tahunanui Beach is Nelson's main beach, with 3 km of golden sand on Tasman Bay. Its waters are among the warmest on the South Island in summer. The sunset over the forested hills of the hinterland reflects in the shallow water of the bay.

⏱ 75 min

Activities

Visit artisan workshops in Nelson: the city is known for its strong artisan community - potters, glassblowers, jewellers and painters have established their studios here. Nelson Arts Gallery and Suter Art Gallery showcase the best of local creation. Bridge Street concentrates many of these studios.

⏱ 90 min

Craft beer tastings: the Nelson region is New Zealand's hop capital - much of the hop used throughout the country is grown here. Several craft breweries offer tastings from the city outskirts.

⏱ 90 min

Where to stay in Nelson

N
$
Book this hotel
Nelson Escape9.7
Budget

Comfortable Living Space: Nelson Escape offers a spacious apartment in Nelson, New Zealand.

Comfortable Living Space: Nelson Escape offers a spacious apartment in Nelson, New Zealand. The property features one bedroom and one bathroom, providing ample space for a relaxing stay. Modern Amenities: Guests enjoy free WiFi, air-conditioning, and a terrace with garden views. The apartment includes a kitchenette, ensuring convenience during the stay. Leisure Facilities: The apartment features a hot tub, perfect for unwinding after a day of exploration. Free on-site private parking is available for those travelling by car. Local Attractions: Trafalgar Park is 6 km away, while Nelson Cathedral lies 7 km from the property. Nelson Airport is located 12 km from Nelson Escape, providing easy access to the region.

The self-catering apartments also include free unlimited WiFi, and a Smart TV. All apartments include a designer kitchenette with dishwasher, microwave and refrigerator. Most feature a balcony or patio and some apartments boast a spa bath. Palazzo Motor Lodge is a 10-minute walk from Trafalgar Park. Nelson Airport and Nelson Golf Club (Nelson Golf Links) are a 15-minute drive away. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.6 for a two-person trip. Delorenzo Studio Apartments offers a heated outdoor pool, on-site parking and free Wi-Fi access. Studios come with a kitchenette, laundry facilities and a private bathroom. Some apartments feature a spa bath. Fully air-conditioned, each studio comes with a flat-screen TV and a balcony with outdoor furniture. The attached kitchenette features a stove, a microwave and tea/coffee making facilities. There is a barbecue area available for guests’ use. Baggage storage is provided. The property offers an in-room dining service for dinner. Studio Apartments Delorenzo is 250 metres from the centre of Nelson. It is a 12-minute drive from Nelson Golf Links and a 15-minute drive from the Nelson Airport.

Day 15

Marahau

Portal to golden coves and turquoise waters edged by native forest
Abel Tasman National Park KayakAnchorage Bay Abel TasmanMarahau Abel TasmanKaiteriteri Beach Abel Tasman
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Abel Tasman National Park is New Zealand's smallest national park (225 km²) and the sunniest. Its golden sand beaches ringed by native forest and turquoise waters are accessible on foot (Coastal Track, one of the country's 10 Great Walks) or from the sea. The park takes its name from Dutch explorer Abel Janszoon Tasman who sailed along the coast in 1642 - he didn't land as his men were killed during first contact with Māori.

⏱ 60 min
2

Anchorage Bay is considered one of Abel Tasman's most beautiful beaches: a sheltered bay of white sand surrounded by dense forest, accessible only on foot (2-3h from Marahau) or by water taxi. Observation Beach cove, accessible at low tide from Anchorage, is even more secluded.

⏱ 120 min

Activities

Sea kayaking in Abel Tasman: this is the park's signature activity. Operators offer day trips or multi-day trips with lodge accommodation. The kayak + water taxi formula allows paddling one way and returning by boat. Fur seals and oyster catchers are common in the coves.

⏱ 240 min

Section of the Coastal Track between Marahau and Anchorage: this roughly 13 km segment follows the coast with constant sea views, crosses estuaries and reaches several almost-deserted intermediate bays. Water taxis pick up walkers at the agreed time.

⏱ 300 min

Where to stay in Marahau

$$

Guests enjoy free WiFi, air-conditioning, a washing machine, and a fully equipped kitchen. Outdoor Spaces: The apartment features a garden with scenic views, perfect for relaxation. A TV provides entertainment options for leisure time. Convenient Location: Marahau Beach is a 9-minute walk away, offering scenic coastal views. Nelson Airport is 62 km from the property, providing easy access for travellers. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

Breakfast includes continental and à la carte selections with pancakes and fruits. Dinner and cocktails are available. Accommodation: Rooms feature private bathrooms, bathrobes, free toiletries, showers, carpeted floors, and wardrobes. Sea, pool, and mountain views enhance the stay. Day 16

Takaka

Bohemian outpost guarding the clearest springs in the southern hemisphere
Farewell Spit Collingwood Golden BayWharariki Beach Cape FarewellTe Waikoropupu Springs TakakaPupu Springs Golden Bay
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Pupu Springs - Te Waikoropupu - are New Zealand's largest freshwater springs and the southern hemisphere's largest cold-water springs. Visibility measurements by NIWA in 1993 recorded 63 m of transparency, approaching optically pure water - only the Weddell Sea in Antarctica does better. For local Māori these waters are wahi tapu (sacred) - swimming, drinking and smoking are strictly forbidden within the precinct. A short loop walk (1 km) follows the river to Dancing Sands spring, which appears to 'dance' from upwellings from the bottom.

⏱ 60 min
2

Farewell Spit is a 35 km sand bar extending into the Tasman Sea from Cape Farewell, the northernmost point of the South Island. It's New Zealand's most strictly protected nature reserve - only the first 4 km are accessible to the public, the rest only via authorised guided tours. The spit hosts several thousand gannets each spring in the country's only sea-level colony, over 90 bird species, and regular sightings of pilot whale strandings.

⏱ 90 min
3

Wharariki Beach, west of Cape Farewell, is perhaps New Zealand's most spectacular beach - not for swimming (dangerous currents) but for its wind-sculpted rock arches and giant dunes crossed on foot from the car park (20 minutes). Fur seals lounge on the rocks at the beach entrance.

⏱ 75 min

Activities

Guided 4x4 tour of Farewell Spit: authorised operators depart daily from Collingwood to cross the dunes, reach the 1869 lighthouse and observe birds at the spit's feeding stations. It's the only legal way to explore the closed portion of the reserve.

⏱ 180 min

Where to stay in Takaka

$

Outdoor Amenities: Guests can enjoy mountain views, an outdoor seating area, and a picnic area. The property features a barbecue, outdoor furniture, and free toiletries. Convenient Facilities: The caravan includes a refrigerator, electric kettle, kitchenware, and a wardrobe. Free toiletries and a shower enhance the stay. 🚗Drive to next stop: 245 km · 3h30
Day 17

Karamea

Where limestone arches tower above rivers older than memory
Oparara Arch KarameaMoria Gate Arch Oparara BasinHoneycomb Hill Caves KarameaKahurangi National Park Karamea
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

The Ōpārara Basin, 20 km north of Karamea in Kahurangi National Park, is one of New Zealand's least-known curiosities. The Ōpārara Arch, the southern hemisphere's largest natural limestone arch, measures 219 m long, 79 m wide and 43 m high - the river carved it through 35-million-year-old limestone over more than a million years. Inside, you walk on fine sand banks while the tea-coloured river (stained by humic acids from the forest) flows silently below. The trail from the car park takes 25 minutes.

⏱ 90 min
2

Moria Gate Arch, 20 minutes' walk from the same car park, is smaller (19 m high, 43 m wide) but its ceiling is completely carpeted with stalactites and hanging roots - the name comes straight from Lord of the Rings (as do several neighbouring streams: Galadriel Creek, Nimrodel). The adjacent Honeycomb Hill caves, accessible only on guided tours, have yielded some of the world's best-preserved moa bones - some dating back 20,000 years.

⏱ 75 min

Activities

Whitebait (galaxiidae) tasting: Karamea is one of New Zealand's last regions where whitebait fishing is still abundant (season September to October). These tiny transparent fish are a national delicacy - whitebait fritters (light egg patties) are available at local cafés like the Karamea Village Hotel.

⏱ 60 min

Hike in the Ōpārara valley: beyond the arches, a 4.5 km one-way trail follows the river through primeval rimu and kahikatea forest - the same trees that populated forests in the dinosaur era. Blue ducks (whio), one of the country's rarest species, breed in the rapids.

⏱ 120 min

Where to stay in Karamea

F
$$
Book this hotel
Fenian Retreat9.7
Mid-range

Comfortable Accommodation: Fenian Retreat in Karamea offers a holiday home with two bedrooms and a bathroom.

Comfortable Accommodation: Fenian Retreat in Karamea offers a holiday home with two bedrooms and a bathroom. The property features a sun terrace and free WiFi, ensuring a pleasant stay. Relaxing Facilities: Guests can enjoy a hot tub, outdoor seating area, and picnic spot. The home includes family rooms, bicycle parking, and free on-site private parking. Local Attractions: Located 107 km from Westport Airport, Fenian Retreat provides easy access to fishing, hiking, and cycling activities. Sea views enhance the surrounding scenery. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 10 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

🚗Drive to next stop: 100 km · 1h30
Day 18

Punakaiki

Where the sea stacks stones like pancakes and erupts through blowholes
Pancake Rocks Blowholes PunakaikiPaparoa National ParkTruman Track PunakaikiDolomite Point Punakaiki
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Punakaiki's Pancake Rocks are New Zealand's most photographed geological formation. These 30-million-year-old limestone pillars - built layer by layer from marine shell debris - were uplifted by tectonics and carved into distinct slices by alternating hard and soft layers. At high tide with a southwesterly swell, the sea surges into underground galleries and shoots through vertical vents in columns of water and spray. The boardwalk circuit is 1.1 km - allow 45 minutes.

⏱ 60 min
2

Truman Track, 3 km north of the Pancake Rocks, is a 30-minute trail through nīkau palm forest (New Zealand's only native palms) emerging onto a spectacular pebble beach with cliffs, caves and marine arches. Less crowded than Pancake Rocks, it's well worth the detour.

⏱ 60 min

Activities

Hike through Pororari Gorge: the Pororari River Track climbs 5 km through limestone gorges overrun with tropical vegetation and tree ferns. The deep green river contrasts with the light rock - one of the West Coast's most remarkable landscapes.

⏱ 180 min

Seal and Hector's dolphin watching from Punakaiki cliffs: Punakaiki Marine Reserve (created 2014, 3,520 ha) protects this exposed coast. Hector's dolphins - the world's smallest and rarest - frequent the area, and fur seals regularly rest on rocks at the cliff base.

⏱ 60 min

Where to stay in Punakaiki

C
$
Book this hotel
Charleston Goldfields Accommodation9.6
Budget

Comfortable Accommodation: Charleston Goldfields Accommodation in Charleston offers a bed and breakfast…

Comfortable Accommodation: Charleston Goldfields Accommodation in Charleston offers a bed and breakfast experience with a garden, terrace, and free WiFi. Free on-site private parking is available for guests. Modern Amenities: Each room features air-conditioning, a kitchenette, private bathroom, sea views, and garden views. Additional amenities include a dining area, outdoor furniture, and a TV. Delicious Breakfast: A continental breakfast is served daily, highly praised by guests for its quality and variety. Convenient Location: Located 29 km from Westport Airport and 33 km from Punakaiki Scenic Attraction, the property provides easy access to local attractions. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.6 for a two-person trip.

Additional amenities include a fully equipped kitchen, fireplace, and outdoor seating and dining areas. Day 19

Franz Josef

Where glaciers plunge into rainforest at impossible altitudes
Franz Josef Glacier terminal face walkLake Matheson Fox GlacierFranz Josef village West CoastCallery Gorge Walk Franz Josef
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Franz Josef Glacier (Kā Roimata o Hine Hukatere) descends from the Southern Alps to below 300 m elevation - one of the few glaciers worldwide to plunge to this level into temperate rainforest. The valley trail (5.4 km return) follows the glacial river bed to the terminal face with its blue ice towers. The final section is on unmarked terrain since glacier modifications - stop at safety barriers. Helicopter flights with glacier landing are the most impressive way to explore it.

⏱ 150 min
2

Lake Matheson, 5 km from Fox Glacier village, is a kettle lake formed 14,000 years ago by Fox Glacier's retreat. The water is stained dark brown by tannins from the surrounding forest - this perfect mirror reflects Aoraki/Mount Cook (3,724 m, New Zealand's highest peak) and Mount Tasman (3,497 m) in optimal conditions. The complete circuit (1h30) passes Reflection Island, the best viewpoint. Sunrise and sunset are prime times.

⏱ 90 min

Activities

Helicopter flight with glacier landing: several operators offer 20 to 45-minute flights over both glaciers with névé landing - the aerial view over the Southern Alps and both ice caps is one of New Zealand's most spectacular.

⏱ 60 min

Soak in Franz Josef hot pools: geothermal pools heated to 36-38°C surrounded by outdoor forest on the village outskirts allow well-deserved relaxation after the day's walking. Private pool option available.

⏱ 90 min

Where to stay in Franz Josef

T
$
Book this hotel
Te Awa Cottages9.3
Budget

Comfortable Accommodation: Te Awa Cottages in Franz Josef offers adults-only holiday homes with free WiFi,…

Comfortable Accommodation: Te Awa Cottages in Franz Josef offers adults-only holiday homes with free WiFi, air-conditioning, and private bathrooms. Each unit features a balcony with mountain views, a dining area, and a sofa bed. Convenient Facilities: Guests enjoy free on-site private parking, a private check-in and check-out service, and an outdoor seating area. Additional amenities include a tea and coffee maker, refrigerator, microwave, and TV. Prime Location: Located 133 km from Hokitika Airport, the property is highly rated for its scenic location and convenient location. Guests appreciate the room comfort and excellent service. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.3 for a two-person trip.

Surrounded by beautiful native trees and bush land, Aspen Court Motel Franz Josef offers guests free Wi-Fi. Opened in December 2013, the property offers heated rooms with a flat-screen TV. It is a 1-minute walk from Glacier Hot Pools. All accomodation offers radiator and underfloor heating. All features double glazed windows, a TV and a kitchenette. Rooms are serviced daily. Some rooms have a spa bath. Aspen Court is a 10-minute drive from the Franz Josef Glacier, and Fox Glacier is a 25-minute drive away. There are a variety of cafes and restaurants within a 5-minute walk. Other facilities include a BBQ, a guest laundry and a booking service for local activities. Guests enjoy free parking. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.6 for a two-person trip.

Day 20

Wanaka

Quieter sister to Queenstown, where a lone tree captures every photographer's heart
Wanaka Lake Central OtagoBlue Pools Makarora Haast PassThunder Creek Falls Haast PassRoy's Peak Wanaka
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Haast Pass (Tioripatea, 562 m) is one of three roads crossing the Southern Alps. Ngāi Tahu used it to reach the West Coast and its pounamu (jade/greenstone) deposits. Geologist Julius von Haast officially explored it in 1863 - he returned to Wanaka 'virtually shoeless' after six weeks' expedition. Along the road, several stops are essential: Blue Pools (glacial turquoise water on suspension bridges, 3 km return), Thunder Creek Falls (96 m drop), and Fantail Falls (23 m) - all accessible in less than 15 minutes' walk from the road.

⏱ 90 min
2

Wanaka sits on the shore of its namesake lake, ringed by mountains at 900 m elevation. It's Queenstown's quieter, more authentic cousin - same access to ski fields (Cardrona, Treble Cone), same mountains, but without the crowds. The 192 km² lake offers direct views of snow-capped peaks. The solitary tree growing at the lake edge is Wanaka's most photographed object.

⏱ 75 min

Activities

Roy's Peak hike: from central Wanaka, this 16 km return trail climbs to 1,578 m with panoramic views over the lake and Alps. The ascent takes about 3h. Leave before dawn for sunrise - it's New Zealand's most shared photograph.

⏱ 360 min

Central Otago wine tasting from Wanaka: the region is the world's southernmost wine zone and produces the country's most renowned pinot noir. Several wine estates are accessible within 20-30 minutes from Wanaka towards Cromwell.

⏱ 120 min

Where to stay in Wanaka

$
Book this hotel

Comfortable Accommodation: Matai Cottage and Studio in Wanaka offers a holiday home with a sun terrace, garden, and free WiFi. The property includes a private bathroom, family rooms, and free on-site private parking. Modern Amenities: Guests can enjoy a fully equipped kitchen, air-conditioning, and a washing machine. Additional amenities include a dishwasher, microwave, and outdoor dining area. Free on-site private parking is available. Local Attractions: Puzzling World and The Wanaka Tree are 3.4 km away, while Cardrona is 37 km from the property. Wanaka Airport is 10 km distant. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.2 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

Day 21

Queenstown

Adventure capital where bungy was born and adrenaline flows eternal
Queenstown Skyline Gondola WakatipuArrowtown Gold Rush VillageQueenstown Waterfront Lake WakatipuQueenstown Gardens
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Queenstown stretches along the shore of Lake Wakatipu, whose Māori name means 'the place of the goblin absorbed' - according to legend, a giant creature sleeps beneath the waters and its breathing makes the lake rise and fall 6 cm every five minutes (a real phenomenon, actually due to hydrodynamic oscillations). The Skyline Gondola climbs 450 m above town - the gradient is the steepest of any cable-supported line in the southern hemisphere. From the summit, the view over the lake, the Remarkables and Cecil Peak is exceptional.

⏱ 90 min
2

Arrowtown, 20 minutes from Queenstown, is a gold rush village preserved intact. Hundreds of miners flocked here after gold was discovered in the Arrow River in 1862. The Lakes District Museum displays relics from this period, and the Arrow River banks remain scattered with traces of old gold-panning channels. In autumn (April-May), the poplars and plane trees turn the Arrow gorge into an unreal landscape of reds and golds.

⏱ 90 min

Activities

Bungy jump from Kawarau Bridge (43 m): this is where the world's first commercial bungy jump took place in 1988. The historic 1880 bridge is now entirely dedicated to the activity with a free spectator platform overlooking. Queenstown also has the Nevis Bungy (134 m, the country's highest) and the Ledge (400 m height on the Skyline).

⏱ 60 min

Jet boat on the Shotover or Kawarau River: flat-bottomed boats powered by jet turbines skim the gorge walls at 85 km/h with 360° turns in a few centimetres of water. Invented in New Zealand by Sir William Hamilton in the 1950s for navigating shallow rivers - Queenstown is its world capital.

⏱ 60 min

Where to stay in Queenstown

T
$
Book this hotel
The Olde Whiskey Room9.9
Budget

Beachfront Location: The Olde Whiskey Room in Queenstown offers direct beachfront access, a sun terrace, and…

Beachfront Location: The Olde Whiskey Room in Queenstown offers direct beachfront access, a sun terrace, and a lush garden. Guests can relax by the bar and enjoy free WiFi throughout the apartment. Comfortable Accommodation: The apartment features one bedroom and one bathroom, equipped with air-conditioning, a kitchenette, and a private entrance. Additional amenities include a patio, barbecue facilities, and free on-site parking. Local Attractions: Located 7 km from Queenstown Airport, the property is close to Queenstown Event Centre (9 km) and Skyline Gondola and Luge (15 km). Activities such as skiing, hiking, cycling, and snorkelling are available nearby. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

Located at the heart of the Central Otago wine region and just a 20-minute drive from Queenstown Airport,…

Located at the heart of the Central Otago wine region and just a 20-minute drive from Queenstown Airport, Gibbston Valley Lodge and Spa offers luxury accommodation, 5-star personal service, an on-site restaurant and a Day spa. The 24 private villas feature views over the organic vineyards and the mountains of Gibbston. All villas are air conditioned and include a spacious and luxurious indoor living space. They open onto a private, secluded, covered deck and sunny courtyard. The large bathroom has a walk-in shower, separate bath and dual basins as well as premium, locally made beauty products. Guests will be able to enjoy a wide variety of experiential packages, tailored to their needs and interests.

Day 22

Te Anau

Gateway to fjords where glow-worms illuminate cathedral caves
Te Anau Glowworm Caves FiordlandLake Te Anau FiordlandTe Anau Glowworm Caves TourFiordland National Park Te Anau
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Te Anau Glowworm Caves are on the west shore of the lake, inaccessible by road - a catamaran crosses the lake from town centre (25 min). The caves, only 12,000 years old, are still forming: an active underground river constantly carves the limestone corridors. You descend on foot through sculpted passages, pass waterfalls and whirlpools, then board a small boat in complete darkness. The ceiling then illuminates with thousands of Arachnocampa luminosa larvae - bioluminescent midges that spin sticky threads to trap prey. Photography forbidden - flash disturbs the organisms.

⏱ 150 min
2

Te Anau is the main base for exploring Fiordland National Park (12,500 km², UNESCO World Heritage listed). Lake Te Anau is the South Island's largest lake and New Zealand's second-largest. The town itself is quiet and friendly - the right place to stock up and review weather forecasts before Milford.

⏱ 60 min

Activities

Hike a section of the Kepler Track from Te Anau: the Great Walk leaves the lake shore 5 km past town. The segment to the ridge lookouts (3-5h return) offers aerial views over the lake and Fiordland mountains without committing to the full 4-day circuit.

⏱ 240 min

Where to stay in Te Anau

$
Book this hotel

Guests enjoy stunning lake, garden, and mountain views. Comfortable Accommodations: Each room features a private bathroom with a walk-in shower, bathrobes, and free toiletries. Guests can relax in the lounge, outdoor seating area, or enjoy the daily housekeeping service.
