Day 23

Te Anau

Gateway to fjords where glow-worms illuminate cathedral caves
Milford Sound Cruise FiordlandMitre Peak Milford SoundMilford Road Homer TunnelMilford Sound Waterfall
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

The Milford Road from Te Anau (120 km) is itself spectacular: Mirror Lakes, Fiordland's alpine landscapes, and Homer Tunnel (1,270 m, hand-carved between 1935 and 1954 under Homer Saddle at 945 m elevation) before the switchback descent to the fiord. Leave early to avoid the buses.

⏱ 90 min
2

The Milford Sound cruise crosses the fiord for 22 km from the wharf to the Tasman Sea mouth. Mitre Peak (1,692 m) rises almost vertically from the water less than a kilometre from the wharf. The captain approaches Stirling Falls (155 m) and Bowen Falls (162 m) close enough to get drenched in spray. Bottlenose dolphins, fur seals and sometimes Fiordland crested penguins appear regularly. Milford Sound receives 7 m of rain per year - the light after a shower on the cliffs is unforgettable.

⏱ 150 min

Activities

Kayaking in Milford Sound: operators offer 3-hour morning outings at water level, beneath the vertical cliffs. You perceive the wall heights on a human scale, and seals let you approach within a few metres. The morning calm contrasts with the cruise boat bustle mid-day.

⏱ 180 min

Where to stay in Te Anau

Day 24

Mount Cook

Where New Zealand's highest peak pierces clouds above milky glacial lakes
Hooker Valley Track Aoraki Mount CookLake Pukaki Mount Cook RoadTasman Glacier Aoraki Mount Cook National ParkAoraki Mount Cook National Park
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Lake Pukaki, on the road to Mount Cook, is one of the world's most spectacular glacial lakes. Its intense turquoise hue is caused by 'glacial flour' - microscopic rock particles suspended in meltwater from Tasman Glacier. The road follows the east shore for 55 km to Mount Cook village, with Aoraki (3,724 m, New Zealand's highest peak) growing with every kilometre.

⏱ 60 min
2

Hooker Valley Track is the most popular hike in Aoraki/Mount Cook National Park. This 10 km return trail (3-4h, easy) crosses three swing bridges over the Hooker River and terminates at Hooker Lake, where icebergs float before the Hooker Glacier terminal face. It's the closest accessible point to the Mount Cook summit. The Tasman Glacier base, the country's longest (29 km), is visible to the side. In spring, the Mount Cook buttercup - the world's largest - blooms along the trail edges.

⏱ 240 min

Activities

Glacier Explorers on Lake Tasman: motorised zodiacs navigate between icebergs in the glacial lake at the foot of Tasman Glacier. The guide explains how the glacier's terminal face has retreated several kilometres since the early 20th century - the lake didn't exist yet in 1973.

⏱ 120 min

Stargazing from Mount Cook village: at 900 m elevation, far from any light pollution, the Mackenzie sky is rated level 2 on the Bortle scale (one of the world's darkest skies). The Milky Way is visible to the naked eye most clear nights.

⏱ 90 min

Where to stay in Mount Cook

A
$$
Book this hotel
Aoraki Court Motel9.2
Mid-range

Aoraki Court Motel is a modern accommodation located in Aoraki Mount Cook National Park.

Aoraki Court Motel is a modern accommodation located in Aoraki Mount Cook National Park. All units feature great views of surrounding mountains and Mount Sefton. There is free on-site parking and free Wi-Fi. The heated guest rooms come with double glazed windows and a flat-screen TV. Each has a patio with mountain views. All rooms also have a microwave, a fridge and kitchenware. Self-service guest laundry is available. The property can provide guests with free hiking maps and daily newspaper. Union Pay credit cards are accepted. Aoraki Court Motel is a 3-minute walk from various dining options at Mount Cook Village. It is 210 metres from Sir Edmund Hillary Alpine Centre and 450 metres from Aoraki Mount Cook National Park Visitor Centre. Aoraki Mount Cook Airport is 6.5 km away.

A
$$
Aoraki Court Motel
9.2Mid-range
🚗Drive to next stop: 105 km · 1h30
Day 25

Lake Tekapo

Where a stone church frames infinity and the Milky Way casts shadows
Church of the Good Shepherd Lake TekapoMount John Observatory Lake TekapoAoraki Mackenzie Dark Sky ReserveLake Tekapo Lupins
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Church of the Good Shepherd, built in 1935 from local stone to honour Mackenzie Basin pioneers, is one of New Zealand's most reproduced photographs. Its small window behind the altar frames the turquoise lake and mountains directly - by day. But at night this church becomes iconic: it's photographed with the Milky Way backdrop from the neighbouring sheepdog memorial. It's an active church - respect services in progress.

⏱ 45 min
2

Aoraki Mackenzie International Dark Sky Reserve covers 4,367 km² including Aoraki/Mount Cook National Park and the Mackenzie Basin. Designated by the International Dark-Sky Association in 2012, it's the world's largest dark sky reserve and the southern hemisphere's only Gold-tier reserve. Level 2 on the Bortle scale means the Milky Way casts shadows in clear weather.

⏱ 45 min

Activities

Guided tour of Mount John Observatory: authorised operators bus visitors (private access closes after 5pm to preserve darkness) to the summit of Mount John (1,029 m) where the University of Canterbury operates New Zealand's only professional research observatory. Guides show southern constellations, visible planets and explain Māori astronomy (tātai aroraki). In cloudy conditions, a VR replacement tour is offered.

⏱ 120 min

Soak in Tekapo Springs hot pools and stargaze lying in the pools: the 'star pool' formula combines guided astronomical observation with outdoor hot bathing at 37°C. It's one of New Zealand's most memorable night experiences.

⏱ 120 min

Where to stay in Lake Tekapo

T
$
Book this hotel
The Mackenzie Suites9.5
Budget

Featuring beautiful lake and mountain views, The Mackenzie Suites offers luxurious accommodation located 600…

Featuring beautiful lake and mountain views, The Mackenzie Suites offers luxurious accommodation located 600 metres from the village of Lake Tekapo. The accommodation is air conditioned and features large terraces at the front and rear of the property. Twizel is 41 km from the property. Each luxury suite is equipped with a kitchenette featuring stone benches, cooking facilities, refrigerator and dishwasher. They have their own dining area. Towels, bathrobes, alpaca wool duvet, bed linen and complimentary slippers are also provided. Some rooms feature a wall gas fire place. Mount Cook Village is 42 km from The Mackenzie Suites, A pick up and drop off service is available for $15 to and from Tekapo Village for guests arriving by bus.

Comfortable Accommodations: Grand Suites Lake Tekapo in Lake Tekapo offers family rooms with private…

Comfortable Accommodations: Grand Suites Lake Tekapo in Lake Tekapo offers family rooms with private bathrooms, air-conditioning, and mountain views. Each room includes a balcony or terrace, a dining area, and modern amenities such as a TV and free WiFi. Exceptional Facilities: Guests enjoy a terrace, free WiFi, and private check-in and check-out services. Additional facilities include a lift, full-day security, express check-in and check-out, luggage storage, and free on-site private parking. Prime Location: Located 96 km from Richard Pearse Airport and 44 km from Mt. Dobson, the hotel is highly rated for its lakes, convenient location, and scenic views. Activities available include skiing and cycling. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.

🚗Drive to next stop: 215 km · 3h05
Day 26

Kaikoura

Where mountains meet ocean and giants surface from the deep
Whale Watch KaikouraKaikoura Peninsula WalkwayPoint Kean Seal Colony KaikouraOhau Point Seal Colony Kaikoura
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Kaikoura - 'eat crayfish' in Māori - sits at the intersection of the Kaikōura Range and an undersea canyon plunging over 1,000 m just kilometres offshore. It's this convergence of cold and warm currents, combined with upwelling from the depths, that attracts a concentration of marine life unique worldwide. The Point Kean fur seal colony is accessible on foot from the car park - some individuals regularly come to inspect parked cars.

⏱ 60 min
2

The Peninsula Walkway circles the Kaikōura peninsula for 11 km (3h, free). The lookouts overlook the undersea canyon where sperm whales regularly surface. It's one of the South Island's most beautiful coastal walks, with views of the Seaward Kaikōura Ranges (the mountains plunge directly into the sea).

⏱ 120 min

Activities

Whale watching cruise with Whale Watch Kaikoura: giant sperm whales are permanent residents at Kaikōura year-round, feeding on giant squid in the canyon depths. Whale Watch Kaikoura, the only boat company of its kind in New Zealand, posts a 95% success rate and refunds 80% of the fare if no whales are sighted. You may also encounter dusky dolphins, orcas and albatrosses depending on season. Book several days ahead in high season.

⏱ 150 min

Swimming with dusky dolphins: morning trips allow plunging with pods of dusky dolphins (groups of 100 to 1,000 individuals). These dolphins are particularly playful and acrobatic - Kaikōura is one of the world's best places for this experience.

⏱ 150 min

Where to stay in Kaikoura

A
$
Book this hotel
Austin Heights - Kaikoura9.5
Budget

Elegant Accommodation: Austin Heights - Kaikoura in Kaikoura offers an adults-only guest house with a garden…

Elegant Accommodation: Austin Heights - Kaikoura in Kaikoura offers an adults-only guest house with a garden and free WiFi. Guests enjoy private check-in and check-out services, an outdoor seating area, and free on-site private parking. Comfortable Amenities: Each room features air-conditioning, a kitchenette, balcony with garden or mountain views, a private bathroom, tea and coffee maker, and a dining area. Additional amenities include a refrigerator, microwave, TV, and free toiletries. Prime Location: Located 140 km from Marlborough Airport, the guest house is a 15-minute walk from Kaikoura Beach. Guests appreciate the scenic views, room cleanliness, and comfort. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.5 for a two-person trip.

Beachfront Location: Bendamere House Bed & Breakfast in Kaikoura offers direct beachfront access with…

Beachfront Location: Bendamere House Bed & Breakfast in Kaikoura offers direct beachfront access with stunning sea views. Guests can relax on the sun terrace or explore the lush garden. Comfortable Accommodations: Rooms feature private bathrooms, air-conditioning, and balconies. Each room includes a kitchenette, terrace, and free WiFi, ensuring a comfortable stay. Delicious Breakfast: A continental buffet breakfast is served daily, including fresh pastries, fruits, and juices. Guests appreciate the quality and variety of the morning meal. Local Attractions: Kaikoura Beach lies less than 1 km away, providing easy beach access. The property is also close to cycling trails and scenic vistas. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 10.

Comfortable Accommodation: The apartment features air-conditioning, a fully equipped kitchen, and a private bathroom with a walk-in shower. Additional amenities include bathrobes, streaming services, and a washing machine. Outdoor Spaces: A spacious terrace provides ample space for outdoor dining, while the garden offers a peaceful retreat. Free WiFi is available throughout the property, ensuring connectivity for all guests.

🚗Drive to next stop: 135 km · 2h
Day 27

Christchurch

Garden city rebuilding itself with cardboard cathedrals and boundless resilience
Akaroa Harbour Banks Peninsula Hector dolphinsBanks Peninsula New ZealandChristchurch Botanic GardensAkaroa French Settlement
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Akaroa, 85 km from Christchurch, is the only town founded by French settlers in New Zealand. In August 1840, 63 French and Germans arrived to colonise Banks Peninsula in France's name - but the British frigate HMS Britomart had preceded them by a few days with the Treaty of Waitangi in hand. The settlers finally established themselves under the British flag. The French memory is everywhere: Rue Jolie, Rue Lavaud, bistros serving bouillabaisse, and even the Tricolour flying from certain windows. Banks Peninsula is the eroded remnant of a volcano extinct 9 million years ago - the harbour is a drowned crater.

⏱ 90 min
2

Akaroa's Hector's dolphins: these dolphins are the world's smallest (up to 1.4 m long) and one of the most endangered species - the South Island subspecies numbers fewer than 15,000 individuals. Akaroa harbour is one of the world's best places to see them. Operators offer nature cruises with over 98% detection rate, and swimming trips with dolphins are also available.

⏱ 120 min
3

Return to Christchurch: arrival late afternoon. If time permits, a pass through the Botanic Gardens (75 ha) and a visit to the Cardboard Cathedral (2013), designed by Shigeru Ban after the 2011 earthquake, show how the city has reinvented itself. Christchurch has the South Island's most convenient international airport for departures.

⏱ 75 min

Activities

Scenic drive on Banks Peninsula coastal roads: from Akaroa, several country roads lead onto the ridges with 360° views over the harbours below. The peninsula is scattered with nearly-deserted bays accessible on foot from the road.

⏱ 90 min

Where to stay in Christchurch

T
$
Book this hotel
The Milano Christchurch9.6
Budget

Situated 15 minutes' walk from Restart City Mall, The Milano Christchurch offers modern accommodation set in…

Situated 15 minutes' walk from Restart City Mall, The Milano Christchurch offers modern accommodation set in garden surrounds. The Milano Christchurch is 5 minutes drive from Hagley Park and Golf Course. Christchurch International Airport is a 15-minute drive away. All rooms are self-contained and feature kitchenettes with coffee and tea making services. All rooms have outdoor seating in front of the unit, on the shared walkway patio/balcony. Some rooms feature a spa bath. All rooms feature a satellite TV with DVD player. There is free on-site parking, with a secure garage available for storage of bikes and motorcycles.

Spacious and Modern: Norton Suite - Christchurch Central offers a spacious, recently renovated apartment in…

Spacious and Modern: Norton Suite - Christchurch Central offers a spacious, recently renovated apartment in Christchurch city centre. The ground-floor unit features a private pool, sauna, fitness centre, bar, and indoor swimming pool. Free WiFi is available throughout the property. Comfortable Amenities: Guests can enjoy a hot tub, washing machine, fully equipped kitchen, and dining area. Additional facilities include a lift, hairdresser/beautician, and free on-site private parking. The apartment is situated on a quiet street with easy access to local attractions. Prime Location: Located 10 km from Christchurch International Airport, the property is close to Christchurch Art Gallery (12-minute walk), Canterbury Museum (1.2 km), and Victoria Square (700 metres).

Additional services include a concierge, daily housekeeping, and a tour desk. Dining Options: The family-friendly restaurant serves brunch and dinner with local specialities, vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free options. Breakfast includes continental, à la carte, full English/Irish, and fresh pastries.
